commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review is needed for the contributor proof gate and for maintainer acceptance of degraded keyword fallback semantics; there is no narrow code repair to queue right now.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the diff touches two memory-core TypeScript files, adds no dependencies or workflows, and uses readMemoryFile() for fallback corpus reads. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a memory-core keyword fallback manager for missing-`node:sqlite` builtin SQLite failures and a regression test for bounded markdown-memory fallback results through safe reads.

PR surface: Source +221, Tests +76. Total +297 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real-runtime reproduction was established in this review. The source path is clear and the test mocks the missing-`node:sqlite` manager failure, but the PR body says the tested local Node has `node:sqlite` available.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed for the contributor proof gate and for maintainer acceptance of degraded keyword fallback semantics; there is no narrow code repair to queue right now.

target.title: fix(memory): fall back to keyword search without sqlite
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: On Node runtimes without the experimental `node:sqlite` builtin, `memory_search` becomes unavailable even though durable markdown memory files still exist locally.
- Solution: Keep the bounded keyword fallback manager for that one runtime capability gap, but route fallback corpus reads through the existing `readMemoryFile` safe memory-file boundary instead of direct `fs.readFile`.
- What changed: The fallback still searches `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`, and configured `extraPaths`, returns bounded snippets with file paths and line ranges, and marks degraded mode in runtime debug metadata. Candidate content is now read through the same safe memory read helper used by `memory_get`/manager read paths.
- What did NOT change: Normal SQLite/vector indexing, QMD behavior, embeddings, provider selection, sync, runtime service APIs, tool schemas, SDK exports, config shape, and file formats are unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users running OpenClaw on Node builds without `node:sqlite` should still get a bounded local markdown-memory recall path instead of losing `memory_search` entirely for a runtime capability gap outside their control.
- **Patch quality notes:** This is a narrow compatibility fallback for the missing-`node:sqlite` manager seam, keeps SQLite/vector search as the canonical path, routes fallback reads through `readMemoryFile`, and fixes the round2 lint/type diagnostics by removing unused fallback bindings without widening behavior.
- Fixes #93150

## Why it matters / User impact

- Users running OpenClaw on a Node build that does not expose `node:sqlite` currently lose `memory_search` completely, even when their durable markdown memories are still present and readable.
- The degraded keyword manager preserves a basic local recall path for existing memory notes instead of surfacing an unavailable-manager response for a runtime capability gap outside the user's control.
- This repair also avoids creating a new filesystem policy path: the fallback now uses the established memory-file read boundary, so workspace and configured `extraPaths` rules remain the source of truth for what memory content can be read.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** When builtin SQLite manager creation fails specifically because `node:sqlite` is unavailable, `memory_search` receives a degraded manager that keyword-searches existing markdown memory artifacts instead of returning unavailable manager metadata. The repaired version no longer opens fallback candidates with direct `fs.readFile`; it uses the safe memory-file read boundary.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw worktree for PR #93260 at commit `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
git diff --check
gh run view 27540476980 --repo openclaw/openclaw --job 81400657733 --log-failed
node -e "try { require('node:sqlite'); console.log('node:sqlite available') } catch (err) { console.log('node:sqlite unavailable:', err.message) }"
ls -1 "$HOME/.nvm/versions/node"
gh search prs "memory_search" --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --json number,title,url,updatedAt --limit 20
gh search prs "node:sqlite" --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --json number,title,url,updatedAt --limit 20
gh search prs "keyword fallback memory" --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --json number,title,url,updatedAt --limit 20
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  36 passed (36)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.30s
```

`extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 37.59s
```

`git diff --check`:

```text
(no output)
```

Round 2 CI diagnostics after first push:

```text
check-prod-types: 'path' is declared but its value is never read.
check-lint: Identifier 'path' is imported but never used; Parameter 'matchCount' is declared but never used.
check-additional-extension-bundled: Identifier 'path' is imported but never used; Parameter 'matchCount' is declared but never used.
```

Round 2 fix verification:

```text
pnpm tsgo:prod: passed core and extensions type checks
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts: no output
pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled: passed
```

Current-head CI preflight attribution:

```text
checkout fetch for '07a1700176e362a97ffbcb2c2cb6ff4804d96ca5' timed out on attempt 1
checkout fetch for '07a1700176e362a97ffbcb2c2cb6ff4804d96ca5' timed out on attempt 2
checkout fetch for '07a1700176e362a97ffbcb2c2cb6ff4804d96ca5' timed out
Process completed with exit code 124
```

Local runtime capability check:

```text
node:sqlite available
v22.22.0
```

Related open PR scan:

```text
memory_search: adjacent open PRs include #92647, #90117, #92833, #90727, #91770, #92065, #92164, #92515, and #93260.
node:sqlite: adjacent open PRs include #69199, #90323, #92164, and #93260.
keyword fallback memory: adjacent open PRs include #92341, #92524, #73976, #62338, and #93260.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The fallback regression now verifies the missing-`node:sqlite` manager seam returns a keyword fallback manager, searching `orbit ramen` returns `memory/preferences.md` with line range metadata and fallback debug metadata, and the fallback search path does not call direct `fs.readFile`.
- **What was not tested:** A true downlevel Node binary that lacks `node:sqlite`; this machine only has Node `v22.22.0`, and that runtime reports `node:sqlite available`. Semantic/vector ranking parity with the SQLite index, session transcript fallback, live runtime-service behavior, and provider calls were also not tested because this patch stays inside memory-core fallback manager resolution and local memory-file reads.
- **Why local runtime-service proof is not the right proof path:** The reviewer request is about a Node runtime capability gap (`node:sqlite` unavailable) in the memory-core manager seam. Starting a local OpenClaw runtime service in this environment would still use Node `v22.22.0` with `node:sqlite available`, so it would not exercise the downlevel-runtime failure mode requested by the review.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-002 / RF-004: Real downlevel-runtime proof remains unavailable locally because only Node `v22.22.0` is installed and it has `node:sqlite`. The PR body states that proof boundary explicitly and explains why local runtime-service proof would not satisfy the requested downlevel-Node behavior.
- RF-003 / RF-007 / RF-011: Fixed. The round2 remote failures were caused by unused fallback bindings: `path` was imported but unused, and `matchCount` was destructured only to remove an internal sorting field. The latest commit removes the unused import and maps the sorted fallback candidate to an explicit `MemorySearchResult`.
- RF-005: Disclosed for maintainer decision. The fallback deliberately changes the missing-`node:sqlite` path from unavailable metadata to bounded keyword results; the PR body identifies that compatibility boundary and keeps SQLite/vector search unchanged when available.
- RF-006: Partially fixed and partially external. The CI-blocking lint/type cleanup is fixed in commit `96d9ef309aead772a74e68e9aaece8021f94f7e2`; contributor-side true downlevel runtime proof remains unavailable locally for the same Node-runtime reason described above.
- RF-008: Passed `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`.
- RF-009: Passed `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`.
- RF-010: Passed `git diff --check` with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression forces `MemoryIndexManager.get()` to throw the wrapped missing-`node:sqlite` error, then verifies the search manager falls back to keyword search over `MEMORY.md` and `memory/*.md`, respects `maxResults`, emits fallback debug metadata, and does not use direct `fs.readFile` for fallback corpus reads.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** It exercises the manager-resolution seam where SQLite initialization fails and the safe read boundary where fallback search opens markdown memory files, without loading provider/embedding runtime paths.

## Patch quality notes

- **Fallback wording in the diff:** The fallback is intentionally narrow: it only activates for errors classified as missing `node:sqlite`, while other manager setup errors still return unavailable metadata instead of being hidden.
- **Default returns:** Empty-result returns are used only when the search request excludes the memory source or when no fallback tokens/files produce a bounded keyword match; they are not used to swallow SQLite initialization failures outside the explicit missing-`node:sqlite` path.
- **Filesystem boundary:** The first revision added a degraded manager but used a direct `fs.readFile` path. This repair moves candidate reads to `readMemoryFile`, keeping workspace, configured `extraPaths`, symlink/path-escape, regular-file, line, and character-limit behavior under the existing memory host boundary.
- **Related open PR scan:** I checked open PRs for `memory_search`, `node:sqlite`, and `keyword fallback memory`. Adjacent PRs cover memory-search timeouts/ranking, CJK keyword scoring, node:sqlite messaging/replacement, and keyword-only hybrid behavior, but none is a broader canonical fix for this exact missing-`node:sqlite` manager-seam fallback plus safe memory-file reads.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change vector indexing, QMD search, provider calls, embedding behavior, session transcript search, public config/schema, SDK exports, runtime APIs, or file formats.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** degraded fallback behavior, local filesystem reads, security-boundary, compatibility, memory search result ranking, availability.
- **Risk explanation:** The fallback only activates for explicit missing-`node:sqlite` errors and only reads memory files through the established memory host read boundary. Other manager setup errors still return unavailable metadata.
- **Why acceptable:** The normal indexed path is unchanged, the fallback is bounded by `maxResults`, snippets are truncated, and result metadata clearly marks keyword fallback mode so callers do not treat it as vector-backed search.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source invariant for `memory_search` is that manager resolution should preserve access to readable durable memory when the indexed backend is unavailable for a known runtime capability gap, while keeping file-access policy centralized in the memory host boundary. Before this patch series, the builtin path treated the missing-`node:sqlite` initialization failure as a generic terminal setup error. The first PR revision added the degraded manager but introduced a second filesystem read path for fallback search candidates.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The repair fixes both sides of that invariant at the manager seam: missing `node:sqlite` now maps to a deliberate degraded keyword manager, and fallback corpus reads are delegated back to `readMemoryFile` instead of a direct file read. That means the availability fix is not a symptom mask around one failing call site; it changes manager resolution for the specific runtime capability gap while preserving the canonical memory-file access boundary.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high after safe-read repair; still waiting on maintainer acceptance of degraded keyword fallback policy and any true downlevel-Node proof they require.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The SQLite/vector index remains the canonical source-of-truth search path when available. The keyword manager is a compatibility fallback for one runtime capability gap and uses existing memory host corpus helpers as the boundary for allowed local files.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Backward compatibility is preserved for runtimes where `node:sqlite` is available. There is no migration, config/schema change, breaking API change, or upgrade-path requirement.
- **Out of scope:** Semantic/vector ranking parity, session transcript search, new public config, new schema fields, runtime service APIs, SDK exports, migrations, and provider/embedding behavior.
target.author: mushuiyu886
